action_scaling_terms.coefficient_requirement: absolute gravitational/curvature coefficient or constraint normalization
action_scaling_terms.reason_no_absolute_anchor: a coefficient with absolute dimensions is required to compare this term to dimensionless sectors
classical four-dimensional gauge scaling is L-neutral after field normalization

action_scaling_terms.coefficient_requirement: Dirac operator action source and mass operator theorem
action_scaling_terms.reason_no_absolute_anchor: massless kinetic normalization does not select L

action_scaling_terms.coefficient_requirement: absolute kinetic coefficient if physical units are demanded
action_scaling_terms.reason_no_absolute_anchor: v5.7 fixes sigma_scale but not the physical length multiplying the normalized cell
alpha_scale=2 and beta_scale=8 fix M_BH/M_star, not M_star

action_scaling_terms.coefficient_requirement: boundary tension or absolute boundary action coefficient
action_scaling_terms.reason_no_absolute_anchor: a boundary area term can fix L only if its absolute coefficient is action-derived

action_scaling_terms.coefficient_requirement: collar density or transition-layer action source
action_scaling_terms.reason_no_absolute_anchor: rho_star is normalized; physical collar width remains L times rho_star
lambda_hat_n/L^2 is not an absolute energy without a fixed physical eigenvalue

action_scaling_terms.coefficient_requirement: chosen integration dimension and coefficient units
action_scaling_terms.reason_no_absolute_anchor: the measure tracks L but does not by itself create a stationary equation

candidate_invariants.name: primordial curvature radius
candidate_invariants.reason: R_hat is dimensionless and L is the unfixed modulus

candidate_invariants.name: compact volume
candidate_invariants.reason: volume scales with L and no equation fixes V_0

candidate_invariants.name: boundary area
candidate_invariants.reason: area is proportional to the unfixed modulus

candidate_invariants.name: integrated scalar curvature
candidate_invariants.reason: no absolute curvature normalization or action quantum is derived
candidate_invariants.rescaling_behavior: rescales with the chosen support dimension

candidate_invariants.name: extrinsic curvature invariant
candidate_invariants.reason: boundary coefficient/tension with absolute units is not derived
candidate_invariants.rescaling_behavior: tracks L through K~1/L and dA~L^2

candidate_invariants.name: collar width
candidate_invariants.reason: rho_star=1 is a coordinate convention, not a physical length

candidate_invariants.name: lowest nonzero spectral eigenvalue
candidate_invariants.reason: lambda_hat_n is dimensionless; no absolute eigenvalue theorem exists

candidate_invariants.name: topological quantization number
candidate_invariants.reason: integer topology can label sectors but cannot supply a length

candidate_invariants.name: action stationary scale
candidate_invariants.reason: current S_eff is flat or depends on free dimensionful coefficients

candidate_invariants.name: regular turning surface
candidate_invariants.reason: regularity removes singular branches but does not select a magnitude for L

claim_boundary: BHSM v5.8 audits the primordial compact-state and global size modulus as possible absolute unit anchors. The current action selects dimensionless ratios M_BH/M_star=1/2 and R_BH/ell_star=2 but does not generate ell_star, M_star, particle masses, gauge couplings, CKM values, rare-B observables, or full BHSM completion.
claim_safe_conclusion: BHSM v5.8 does not generate an absolute unit anchor. The present primordial compact-state candidate and normalized action preserve a continuous global size modulus unless a new BHSM-native scale-modulus action source, absolute action quantum, boundary tension, spectral eigenvalue, or nonlinear backreaction theorem fixes L.
conditionally_established: redshift can transport a BHSM-native scale after it exists
CMB/relic matter are physical relics, while boundary/topographic memory may be effective or virtual
M_BH/M_star=1/2 and R_BH/ell_star=2 remain valid relative ratios
derived: primordial compact-state candidate in normalized Berger-Hopf variables
action scaling audit for current v5.4-v5.7 terms
proof that v5.7 sigma_scale=1/2 remains a relative scale only

primordial_state.boundary_or_turning_surface: regular compact-to-expanding transition hypersurface in normalized variables
primordial_state.collar_or_transition_layer: rho in [0,1] in normalized coordinates; physical width is L times dimensionless width
primordial_state.geometry: compact normalized Berger-Hopf boundary cell Sigma_B with collar/turning layer [0,rho_star]

late_universe_doctrine.effective_or_virtual_memory: present boundary/topographic response memory, not on-shell relic matter

primordial_state.metric: g(L)=L^2 g_hat with dimensionless Berger metric g_hat
primordial_state.topology: Hopf-fibered compact boundary topology retained as dimensionless topological data
primordial_state.white_hole_like_interpretation: The primordial hot plasma is treated as the outward high-energy phase of an initial compact geometric release. This is an interpretation of the candidate initial data, not an additional equation that fixes L.

primordial_to_late_time_map.initial_release: candidate compact geometric release from normalized primordial boundary data
primordial_to_late_time_map.redshifted_relics: physical on-shell relic matter/radiation may be redshifted from an already-defined initial scale
primordial_to_late_time_map.scale_transport: redshift can transport a scale after ell_star exists; it cannot create ell_star
primordial_to_late_time_map.virtual_or_effective_memory: late boundary/topographic response memory can be effective or virtual

propagation.collar_thickness: rho_star L is physical only after L=ell_star is fixed
propagation.fermion_operator: D_phys = M_BH D_hat only after M_BH exists; no fermion masses are derived
propagation.gauge_operator: L_gauge,phys = M_BH^2 L_gauge,hat only after M_BH exists; alpha_i remains action-gated
propagation.hessian_green_operator: G_phys = M_BH^-2 H_hat^-1 only after M_BH exists
propagation.neutral_response: neutral physical eV/GeV scale remains open
propagation.scalar_spectrum: H_ST,phys = M_BH^2 H_ST,hat only after M_BH exists

scale_modulus.S_eff_current: S_eff(L)=S_hat[sigma_scale=1/2] after factoring the arbitrary unit ell_star
scale_modulus.infinite_size_runaway: flat direction rather than bounded finite minimum
scale_modulus.stationary_points: all positive L are stationary in the normalized reduced model
scale_modulus.why_no_anchor: Every serious candidate either rescales with L, is dimensionless topological data, or requires an unproved absolute action coefficient, boundary tension, action quantum, or spectral eigenvalue.
scale_modulus.zero_size_branch_handled: L=0 is excluded as degenerate geometry, not selected

spectral_topological_assessment.action_stationarity_reason: dS_eff/dL=0 is identically flat or depends on missing dimensionful coefficients
spectral_topological_assessment.regularity_reason: finite action, smooth turning surface, and zero canonical momentum are homogeneous constraints in the current normalized model
spectral_topological_assessment.spectrum_reason: lambda_n=lambda_hat_n/L^2 leaves L arbitrary without an absolute eigenvalue theorem
spectral_topological_assessment.topology_reason: topological charges and Hopf data are dimensionless

unit_anchor.hbar_c_status: conversion convention only; hbar=c=1 may be used as natural-unit convention
unresolved_constants: absolute action coefficient or scale-modulus source

v5_9_pilot_wave_update.claim_boundary: pilot-wave guidance may describe expansion but does not generate ell_star, M_star, M_BH, or R_BH
v5_9_pilot_wave_update.scale_modulus_result: pilot-wave dynamics is scale-covariant; finite L0 is not selected
